reasoning:
The asset experienced an 8% downward correction from mid-November through Nov 21, largely driven by macroeconomic and sector-specific headwinds — specifically, the prospect of increased scrutiny on defense budgets via the newly announced government efficiency efforts (DOGE). This recent momentum is robustly negative, pulling the short-term probability (Nov 30) below 0.5, aligning closely with the yfinance anchor of 0.44 - 0.48.

However, L3Harris's core fundamentals remain strong; the company recently beat Q3 earnings expectations and raised guidance for the year, signaling underlying business health. As the forecast horizon extends to December and February, standard positive market drift and the tendency for stocks to mean-revert after sharp news-driven corrections should dominate. While the DOGE uncertainty acts as an overhang, federal defense cuts are historically slow and difficult to enact. Therefore, I scale my probabilities upward over time, returning to the historical base rate of ~0.53 for the 3-month horizon.
